title: Global name registration — global:register_name and conflict resolution
purpose: The global module provides cluster-wide name registration — a name atom maps to exactly one PID across all connected nodes. Registration uses a leader election protocol to resolve conflicts when the same name is registered on multiple nodes simultaneously.
task: Implement global name registration with a simple conflict resolution strategy.

requirements.title: Global name registry
requirements.spec: A distributed name registry that maps atom names to PIDs across the cluster.
acceptance: global:register_name(Name, Pid) registers the name cluster-wide
global:whereis_name(Name) returns the PID (local or remote) or undefined
Registration propagates to all connected nodes
Test: register on node A, whereis on node B returns correct PID

requirements.title: Conflict resolution
requirements.spec: When the same name is registered on two nodes simultaneously (network partition recovery), resolve the conflict.
acceptance: Default resolution: the registration with the lower node name wins (deterministic)
Losing PID is notified (exit signal or callback)
Custom resolve function support via global:register_name/3
Test: register same name on two nodes, reconnect, verify one registration survives

boundaries: SHALL NOT implement the full global lock protocol — use a simplified single-leader approach
SHALL handle node disconnection by cleaning up registrations for PIDs on the disconnected node
verification: cargo test -p beamr passes with all tests green
